SBB cockpit mockup

Shared Sportsbook BrowserDual-lane execution cockpit

A shared execution surface designed to move from opportunity intake to ready-to-confirm state while keeping one sportsbook per window and the final irreversible click in Noel's hands.

Operator feed

Opportunity accepted from OddsBlaze

2

4

5

Human boundary preserved at final Place Bet click

Design intent
Session truth
Open tabs are not enough. The UI needs to show whether a book is truly reusable.
Lane discipline
One sportsbook per visible window during active execution.
Human boundary
Maximum automation up to the final Place Bet click.
Human boundary
Automation stops at Ready to Confirm. Final Place Bet click is human-only.
Behavior layer
Interactive cockpit controls

Drive lane state, readiness, and session flags locally. Nothing here submits a wager; it only models execution posture up to ready-to-confirm.

Evaluated pair readiness
Derived from lane truth + operator blockers
PAIR RED

Both books blocked - draftkings: Browser not attached; betmgm: Browser not attached

Shared Sportsbook Browser (SBB)

Session Attachment Control Panel

DraftKings
Status: unattached
BetMGM
Status: unattached
BetRiversConservative
Status: unattached
theScoreConservative
Status: unattached

Pair Readiness

DraftKings ↔ BetMGM
Both books blocked - draftkings: Browser not attached; betmgm: Browser not attached
BetRivers ↔ theScore
Both books blocked - betrivers: Browser not attached; thescore: Browser not attached
DraftKings ↔ BetRivers
Both books blocked - draftkings: Browser not attached; betrivers: Browser not attached
BetMGM ↔ theScore
Both books blocked - betmgm: Browser not attached; thescore: Browser not attached
👤

Human in the Loop

This control panel prepares sessions for bet placement, but the final "Place Bet" click is always performed by you. SBB stops at "ready to confirm" — never auto-places bets.

When both books show green (ready), you control the timing and execution of the actual wager.
